programme.title: Afonydd Gwaedlyd
programme.desc: Part two of two. When Niemans obtains X-ray images of the damaged frescos, they provide further insight into the case. Camille accompanies Blandine on a visit to a countryside hospital for the children of the religious community, but fails to realise that she is getting into danger. The police deduce that the murders are connected with an ancient effort by the sect to attain purity, but as they rush to the chateau, will it be all too late for Camille? programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Leicester City v Arsenal at the King Power Stadium and Chelsea v Crystal Palace at Stamford Bridge. The Foxes won last season's corresponding fixture 3-0, with second-half goals from Youri Tielemans and a Jamie Vardy brace securing all three points for Brendan Rodgers' side. Plus, action from Burnley v West Ham United at Turf Moor, Newcastle United v Bournemouth at St James' Park, Southampton v Everton at St Mary's Stadium, Tottenham Hotspur v Sheffield United at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, and Friday night's fixture between Norwich City and Watford at Carrow Road(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Remembrance Sunday
programme.desc: David Dimbleby presents live coverage of the ceremony in Whitehall, with the Queen watching proceedings from the balcony of the Foreign and Commonwealth Office as the Prince of Wales lays a wreath on behalf of the nation. On Horse Guards Parade, Sophie Raworth hears stories of courage and sacrifice from those taking part(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Thomas Plant help two teams find items of value at the Three Counties Showground in the spa town of Malvern, Worcestershire. Presented by Eric Knowles(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins marks Remembrance Sunday by visiting the National Memorial Arboretum near Lichfield in Staffordshire to see how its tranquil surroundings bring comfort and hope to those who have lost loved ones in the armed forces. Sean Fletcher chats to Chelsea Pensioner and Britain's Got Talent Winner Colin Thackery, who performs a special arrangement of the inspirational song You Raise Me Up(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents more updates, scores and goals from today's first-round fixtures(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Highlights of the programme in which Kate Humble and Ben Fogle report from behind the scenes at Longleat Safari Park in Wiltshire, featuring the vets trying to save a binturong with a mystery illness, and Jean Johansson meets the baby meerkats(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to life in Asia, the planet's most varied and extreme continent, which stretches from the Arctic Circle to the equator. Walruses gather in huge numbers in the frozen north and brown bears roam around remote Russian volcanoes, while the deep jungle provides sanctuary for the last few Sumatran rhino. There is also a glimpse of rarely seen creatures, from yeti-like golden snub-nosed monkeys in the mountain forests of China to a bizarre predator in the baking deserts of Iran(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker is with the Essex Wildlife Trust on Fingringhoe Wick - a newly created stretch of saltmarsh - as he helps the team do its first-ever winter fish survey, Margherita Taylor hears about a project to preserve the spirit of the Dig for Victory campaign, before joining Adrian Thomas from the RSPB to learn about the organisation's new sound archive. Adam Henson meets a farmer feeding his cows charcoal, and Tom Heap asks if enough is being done in rural areas to support veterans(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in South America, where animals must specialise if they are to carve out a niche. In Patagonia, a puma mother draws on a lifetime's experience to catch prey three times her weight, while rarely seen Andean bears clamber 30 metres into the cloud forest canopy to find elusive fruit. Poison dart frogs use ingenious methods to keep their tadpoles safe, anacondas stalk capuchin monkeys, and swifts make death-defying flights through Igauzu Falls on the border between Argentina and Brazil(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores taking part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. With music by West End star Luke Evans and a group dance marking Remembrance Sunday(n)

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra leaves the familiar surroundings of Jordan College and Oxford behind as she and Pantalaimon settle into their new life in London, but she is determined to try to find Roger and hopes that Mrs Coulter will be able to help in her mission. Meanwhile, Farder Coram and the Gyptians continue their search for the missing children and the elusive and mysterious Gobblers. Jack Thorne's adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen, Ruth Wilson and James Cosmo(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: On a Polish mission as part of the SOE, Harry grasps a second chance at saving Kasia from the horrors of Warsaw, while new mother Lois is surprised to be visited again by Vernon. Robina continues to appreciate her unlikely and surprising bond with Douglas and Jan, while Nancy promises Webster she will do all she can to help Albert(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including the top-of-the-table clash between Liverpool and Manchester City at Anfield. The Citizens pipped Liverpool to the title by one point last season, but it was the Reds who had set the pace so far this term. Plus, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Aston Villa, Manchester United v Brighton & Hove Albion and a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Motorcycling great Carl Fogarty, England netball star Eboni Usoro-Brown, Olympic hockey gold medallist Helen Richardson-Walsh and world champion swimmer Mark Foster join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood spends 24 hours sampling some of the US's most dangerous and demanding jobs, beginning by working as a correctional officer in a Texas jail(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell looks round a terraced house in Horden, Co Durham and a grade II listed building in Whitehaven, Cumbria. Dion Dublin is in Evesham, Worcestershire(n)

programme.title: Two Minutes Silence
programme.desc: Pause to remember those who have given their lives in war(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A bogus injury scam is foiled as football supporters fail to see the CCTV cameras on board a bus, and a bride-to-be comes unstuck over a claim for a lost engagement ring(n)

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: The programme follows police as they seize a Mercedes from a drug dealer who had not paid back the money he made from crime. Angellica Bell and Rav Wilding present(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: An ex-soldier struggling with motherhood puts Emma in a difficult position rather than tell her partner how she really feels, while Jimmi has some advice for a troubled Daniel(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which contestants must choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Leicestershire to help a couple look for a property on a £750,000 budget, and pays a visit to an arts and crafts cottage that is a time capsule from a bygone era(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and the team transforms items reclaimed from a tip in Witley, Surrey, including a copper hopper funnel, a bedside table and an old mirror and washstand(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and James Braxton battle for profits among the orchards of Kent, before heading to an auction in Washington, West Sussex(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, with former US First Lady and Secretary of State Hillary Clinton(n)

programme.title: The Great Staycation
programme.desc: Mathew Baynton narrates a documentary following the fortunes of a luxury boutique hotel in the Essex town as owners Clare and Javed try to pull off a successful bank holiday. Fully booked, short on staff and with a heatwave looming, tensions are rising in this family-run operation. But will the visitors love it or loathe it?(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The day of Louise and Sharon's party is here and Mel is determined that everything goes to plan. Sharon panics when Phil calls to say he is on his way back to Walford having heard about his wife's plan to sell the club. Mick and Linda worry as they take Ollie to hospital for his assessment, and Karen gets her hopes up when Keegan reveals he and Tiffany are moving out of Jack's. Jean struggles to contain her emotions as she opens up to Shirley about losing Daniel(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Justin Rowlatt investigates the aviation industry's plans to reduce carbon emissions and help save the planet, asking whether it is promising more than it is delivering. With cheap flights leading to a boom in passenger numbers, he hears claims that the industry is putting growth and profits before the environment(n)

programme.title: Gary Lineker: My Grandad's War
programme.desc: The Match of the Day presenter retraces the footsteps of his grandfather Stanley Abbs to explore a brutal but often overlooked chapter of the Second World War. Using the official war diary kept by Stanley's unit as a guide, Gary revisits the places where he was stationed and meets experts who help him piece together what happened to his grandfather during the Italian campaign. Along the way, Gary meets veterans who share first-hand testimonies to and help him understand the reality of combat in Italy(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Jock misses the first scan of his and Siobhan's baby, so her parents come up with a plan to adopt the child. The suggestion doesn't go down well with anyone, so to test Jock's ability to look after a child, Principal Walsh challenges him to take care of an egg for a full month - without losing or breaking it. Return of the comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Citizen Khan star Adil Ray hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Ivo Graham and journalist Helen Lewis joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host chats to Oscar-winner Olivia Colman and Helena Bonham Carter, who are joining Netflix drama The Crown as the Queen and Princess Margaret respectively. Black Panther star Chadwick Boseman discusses his role in new action thriller 21 Bridges, while comedian and actor Richard Ayoade talks about his book Ayoade on Top. Plus, Niall Horan performs Nice to Meet Ya(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood takes on his toughest challenge yet, working as a paramedic in Austin, Texas. His mentor is Cassi Lydon, a veteran paramedic with 14 years' service(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a two-bedroom flat with rental potential in Catford, a terraced house in Dover, and a quirky terraced house in Accrington(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A driver's personal injury claim is rumbled when CCTV footage reveals that he was never even in the car, and an opportunistic bus passenger comes unstuck(n)

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: A massive police operation in Liverpool leads to a series of arrests of criminals, all of whom have made money illegally, but now the police want it back. We also see how Asset Recovery teams in the South West arrest a drugs gang and then seize the designer contents of their wardrobe - literally taking the shirts off their backs. Plus we meet the boxing club in Tuebrook who have had funding from money local police took off criminals(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Kate Bliss help two teams look for items in Stamford Meadows, Lincolnshire, while Natasha Raskin Sharp has advice on how to identify different kinds of wood(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Karen prepares for a risky future, Daniel realises he cannot move forward without Zara's help, and Ayesha discovers her cousin Kayleigh is trying to step into her shoes(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants Yasmin, Miki and Andrew must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is on property-hunting mission with a couple who have a £750k budget to make a move from the Essex coast to the Suffolk countryside(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues an old chimney pot, a wooden chest and a discarded bed, items that she and two other recycling experts restore before she tries to sell them for a profit(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and James Braxton travel to Sussex, Surrey and Hampshire to browse for items that they can sell for a profit at an auction in Wokingham(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Conservative Party(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As the party continues, Sharon and Mel threaten to reveal each other's big secrets. While Lisa tries to make Mel see sense, Sharon sneaks out to meet Phil at the airport. Across the square, Chantelle bumps into Denise but is left confused when she mentions a fight Gray was involved in, and Linda and Mick take a trip down memory lane(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: A paranoid Jac feels she is the centre of hospital gossip when word spreads Emma is now living with Jonny. Elliot's concern for her grows, but feels unable to tell her his secret. Lofty resolves to put his new family first, but even the basics of parenting stir up trouble between him, Helen and Dom. Sacha struggles with his feelings for Essie, which seem doomed to go unrequited. Guest starring Vicky Entwistle (Coronation Street)(n)

programme.title: Gold Digger
programme.desc: Drama, starring Julia Ormond as a wealthy middle-aged woman who begins a relationship with a man nearly 20 years younger than she is just as her divorce is finalised. When he is introduced to her three adult children, suspicions are raised about his motivations. The action cuts between the early days of their relationship and the wedding day, which seems fated to end in disaster. With Ben Barnes and Jemima Rooper(n)

programme.title: Love and Hate Crime
programme.desc: Two murders in Houston, Texas, that left baffled police thinking there was an Iranian hit squad at work. Ali Isran, who was convicted of the crimes, tells his story from death row, and the film follows the extraordinary twists and turns of the case(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fifth episode, Jared and Sophie, a couple from Essex, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: The Great Chelsea Garden Challenge
programme.desc: The four remaining contestants head to Painswick Rococo Garden in the heart of the Cotswolds, where they have to create conceptual gardens that showcase their creativity and impress the judges. James Alexander-Sinclair and Ann-Marie Powell then decide which three go forward to the final. Joe Swift presents(n)

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don heads to India, where he takes in some of the most masterful examples of horticultural and architectural extravagance in the world - including the gardens of the Taj Mahal. Along the way, the green-fingered presenter investigates the majestic tomb gardens of the Mughal emperors, and a unique rock sculpture garden created from discarded objects and rubbish(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team looks at all things autumnal, with Ellie Harrison on Alderney where autumn storms have washed up seaweed that is providing a seasonal feast for an unexpected animal. John Craven takes a walk in the footsteps of John Keats to explore the landscape that inspired his famous poem To Autumn, while Anita Rani is on the farm of Michelin-starred chef Tommy Banks to get some hints on pickling and preserving autumn produce(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef takes a culinary tour of France to discover what Britain's nearest neighbours have to teach us. He begins his trip in Dieppe, where he prepares freshly caught turbot, forages for sea herbs along the Bay of the Somme, and cooks a seafood gratin. He then heads for southern Champagne, a region that was a favourite of Renoir(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The six strongest chefs from the heats compete in the first quarter-final, inventing a dish with rice as the key ingredient. After the weakest two are sent home, the others cook two courses for three critics - William Sitwell, Grace Dent and Jimi Famurewa. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in the next round(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents updates, scores and goals from today's first-round fixtures(n)

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Novak Djokovic v Matteo Berrettini. Coverage of day one from The O2 in London, where the opening singles round-robin match takes place. The season-ending tournament will see the top eight available singles players in the world compete for this prestigious title, which was won last year by Alexander Zverev when he defeated Djokovic in the final(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents action from the weekend's first-round ties, including Dover Athletic v Southend United at Crabble Athletic Ground, Barnet v Fleetwood Town at The Hive Stadium and Chippenham Town v Northampton Town at Hardenhuish Park, as the non-league teams looked to produce upsets against their Football League opponents. With analysis from Kevin Kilbane and Dion Dublin(n)

programme.title: Remembrance Sunday
programme.desc: Coverage of this morning's commemorations from the Cenotaph in Whitehall, as the Queen, members of the Royal Family and the Prime Minister gathered for a two-minute silence in memory of the service personnel who gave their lives in both World Wars and later conflicts(n)

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: After three months at sea, the test pilots from the Royal Navy and RAF have successfully landed the F35 jets on the flight deck of HMS Queen Elizabeth for the first time, and even tested launching and landing in hurricane conditions. But with just a few weeks to go before the warship arrives in New York in spectacular style, they are about to try a method of landing never attempted before, which carries great risks but could revolutionise carrier operations globally. Narrated by Caroline Catz(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels across Central America, from the Caribbean to the Pacific. Along the way, he goes diving on a coral reef on the coast of Belize, and witnesses a gun battle between park rangers and intruders in Chiquibul National Park. He also discovers how climate change has left Guatemala devastated by famine and drought, reports on measures to curb gang violence in El Salvador, and learns how Costa Rica aims to become the world's first carbon neutral country by 2021(n)

programme.title: They Shall Not Grow Old (2018)
programme.desc: Peter Jackson's documentary exploring the First World War, edited from rarely seen archive footage from the Western Front which has been restored and colourised, while lip readings of the men featured in the footage have allowed their speech to be reconstructed. The film also includes archive interviews with servicemen from the BBC and the Imperial War Museum(n)

programme.title: Cycling
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents the concluding action from the second round of the season at the Sir Chris Hoy Velodrome in Glasgow. With analysis from Chris Hoy, and commentary by Simon Brotherton and Chris Boardman(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Glasgow, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three detached properties in Leicestershire are in the spotlight, featuring a retro family home, a country-style new-build and a house with some seriously modern twists(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The 1000th episode of the medical drama sees Jac performing a milestone heart-transplant procedure. However, her personal demons threaten to cloud her judgement, while an old friend returns and is shocked by how much she has changed. The hospital celebrates its 150th birthday with a bonfire party, and Fletch arranges a show-stopping event with some very special guests. Joy is hard to find in the post-op ward, where Ric is still recovering from his brain operation and fears he will never be the same man again(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate how technology enables people to be caught out by elaborate online romance fraud(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts, as experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell help two teams find items of value in Nottingham to take to auction in Sheffield. Anita also brings along a mystery object that has people scratching their heads(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Wildlife documentary observing Professor David Scheel as he takes an octopus into his home to learn about its intelligence, and the extraordinary relationship he and his daughter develop with the creature. Named Heidi, the octopus is seen unravelling puzzles, recognising individual humans and even watching TV with the family. The film also looks at the remarkable behaviour of other octopuses around the world, from those that can change their colour and texture in a split second to the octopus that carries around its own coconut shell to hide in(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Exploring the life of the actor, who died at the age of 83, using archive interviews with the BBC and contributions from director David Lean(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms marks the approach of Oscar-winnning actress Jane Fonda's 80th birthday with a retrospective on her successful - if sometimes controversial - career in Hollywood(n)

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Daniil Medvedev v Stefanos Tsitsipas. Coverage of the afternoon session on day two from The O2 in London, featuring the latest singles match in the round-robin stage. In the corresponding session of last year's tournament, eventual champion Alexander Zverev won two tie-breakers against Marin Cilic to prevail in straight sets(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jay Blades, Shaun Keaveny, Rachel Parris and Jan Ravens test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal is joined by the latest couple to be eliminated from the competition after losing the dance-off. Plus, in-depth analysis of the weekend's performances(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents live coverage of the draw, as the remaining League One, League Two and non-league sides await their fate(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: On the east coast of Ireland, the crew of Courtown's inshore lifeboat face one of their most challenging rescues ever, trying to keep a teenage girl with suspected spinal injuries immobile and afloat for 40 minutes, knowing any movement could leave her paralysed for life. The Torbay crew have just minutes to find a capsized kayaker before she's dashed against the rocks, and just around the coast in Dartmouth, an 81-year-old sailor, fallen overboard in the middle of the night, is left clinging to a rope for his life(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts as first-round losers the Wickets and the Electrophiles return for one last chance to stay in the competition and reach the second stage. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Plastic credit card, Hollywood film industry, Crown monarchy, and Westminster parliament(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions in the second and final of the highest-scoring loser matches, as two sets of students get a last opportunity to earn a place in the next stage of the competition(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: For the last decade, a team of frontline medics has been fighting to save Borneo's critically endangered orangutans. Armed with cameras, the members of International Animal Rescue have documented their struggle as they pull apes from devastated jungles, give emergency medical care, rehabilitate and release the healthiest orangutans back into the wild. This documentary tells both the story of their work and how the orangutan - one of man's closest wild relatives - has been pushed to the brink of extinction(n)

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Julia struggles to abide by the rules and clashes with Mrs Lamb as she attends her first-ever school sports day, where apparently it's no longer acceptable to cheer for individual children and spectators can only offer generic support like 'good job'. Over at the Kidiverse soft-play centre, Kevin is devastated that an important work presentation means he will miss the dads' race. Liz worries that her new boyfriend is blanking her, and Anne discovers something that leads to a nasty surprise for Amanda(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: WWI: The Final Hours
programme.desc: On November 8, 1918, an Englishman, a Frenchman and a German gathered in secret on a train carriage in a forest near Paris. They had met with the intention of hammering out conditions for the Germans' surrender - and after three days, the Armistice was signed. Here, historians examine the meeting from the perspectives of the three key players - German politician Matthias Erzberger, English naval officer Admiral Rosslyn Wemyss and French commander Marshal Ferdinand Foch - and how events unfolded(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The business-based reality show returns. A new group of 16 hopeful candidates embark on their first challenge of the competition. The contestants are sent 6,000 miles from London to the capital city of Cape Town in South Africa, where they are tasked to set up and run their own safari and vineyard tours. One team's brash approach to ticket sales causes commotion, while on the other team premium prices prove problematic(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team looks at all things autumnal, with Ellie Harrison on Alderney where autumn storms have washed up seaweed that is providing a seasonal feast for an unexpected animal. John Craven takes a walk in the footsteps of John Keats to explore the landscape that inspired his famous poem To Autumn, while Anita Rani is on the farm of Michelin-starred chef Tommy Banks to get some hints on pickling and preserving autumn produce(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood spends 24 hours sampling some of the US's most dangerous and demanding jobs, beginning by working as a correctional officer in a Texas jail(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Michael Baggott are at Grittleton House in Wiltshire, where items include a couple of boomerangs and a silver Chinese tea strainer with a jade handle(n)

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ventures Down Under to explore Australia by train. He begins by riding the legendary Ghan Railway, a train almost a kilometre in length and hauled by two locomotives, which covers nearly 2,000 miles across Australia's red centre. Michael traces its route from the southern harbour of Port Augusta to Darwin on the north coast, along the way meeting the indigenous people of Alice Springs, visiting a kangaroo sanctuary and joining Australians to mark the anniversary of Anzac Day, the First Word War battle that defined their nation(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms introduces profiles of Bette Davis and Joan Crawford, the feuding stars who battled over roles, accolades and audience appreciation during Hollywood's 'Golden Age'(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A profile of the movie actor, who died in September 2008. Newman won an Academy Award for his performance in the 1986 Martin Scorsese-directed film The Color of Money, and was nominated eight further times in his career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day three from The O2 in London, featuring a singles match in the round-robin stage. South Africa's Kevin Anderson produced a dominant display to overwhelm Kei Nishikori of Japan 6-0, 6-1 in this session 12 months ago(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The valuation day venue is Muncaster Castle in Cumbria, where experts James Lewis and Adam Partridge are out in force to find the best antiques and collectibles. Plus, Paul Martin finds out about the legendary Tom Fool, the court jester who lived at the castle and caused mayhem during some of its most turbulent times(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jay Blades, Shaun Keaveny, Rachel Parris and Jan Ravens test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal chats to the couple who avoided elimination in Sunday night's dance-off, and previews the songs the couples will be dancing to this weekend in Blackpool(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Dr Ranj Singh and Dr Zoe Williams go looking for antiques in Lancashire and Greater Manchester, aided by experts Stephanie Connell and Tim Medhurst. Ranj visits a Blackpool dance hall and Zoe joins the crew of a steam train before attending an auction in Harrogate, where they hope to make a profit on their purchases(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the latest heat, Monica Galetti challenges three of the chefs to employ deft knife skills to spatchcock a quail before cooking it with rocket pesto in just 15 minutes. The remaining contestants prepare Marcus Wareing's beignets with chocolate sauce, before all six get the chance to redeem any earlier mistakes when they cook their own signature dish for Monica, Marcus and Gregg Wallace. The judges then decide which three have done enough to make it through to the quarter-final stage of the competition(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef continues his 'journey without maps', leaving the main routes and tourist hotspots behind to embark on a personal road trip along picturesque and remote backroads. Here, he heads through France's eastern border regions of Alsace and Jura - famous for Comte cheese and home to his favourite French dish of choucroute garni. He then begins to head inland, visiting Burgundy for a memorable glass of Auxey-Duresses wine, and sampling oeuf en meurette with a difference. Following a memorable Sunday lunch near the French/Swiss border, he is inspired to recreate a traditional blueberry tart(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Sindhu Vee hosts this episode of the comedy showcase recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, featuring stand-up performances by New York-based Edinburgh comedy award winner Alex Edelman, and at the Edinburgh Festival Fringe Comedians' Choice Award winner Lou Sanders(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories with Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: NFL This Week
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present action from the 10th round of fixtures, including Seattle Seahawks v San Francisco 49ers(n)

programme.title: Cycling
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents action from the second round of the season at the Sir Chris Hoy Velodrome in Glasgow. With analysis from Chris Hoy, and commentary by Simon Brotherton and Chris Boardman(n)

programme.title: Dan Cruickshank's Monuments of Remembrance
programme.desc: The extraordinary stories behind First World War cemeteries and memorials, including those at the Somme and Gallipoli, as well as those responsible for creating them(n)

programme.title: Everything Is Connected - George Eliot's Life
programme.desc: In this Arena programme, contemporary artist Gillian Wearing celebrates the legacy of Victorian novelist George Eliot. Just as Eliot's novel Middlemarch explored the lives of ordinary men and women, this experimental film is made up of a diverse cast of people from different backgrounds, and features Jason Isaacs and Sheila Atim as the narrators(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The Rosetta mission to comet 67P was the first time a spacecraft landed on a comet's surface. Cameras reveal what this icy body taught us about the dawn of the solar system and the origins of life on Earth(n)

programme.title: Palace for the People
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the hidden secrets within five of communism's most splendid palaces through the eyes of the people who designed, built and worked in them(n)

programme.title: Great War Horses
programme.desc: An account of the men and horses of the Australian Light Horse regiments, and the pivotal role they played in the First World War at the Battle of Romani in 1916, the Light Horse charge at the Battle of Beersheba in 1917, and the capture of Damascus in 1918. Bred for Australia's tough Outback conditions, the horses, known as Walers, were well equipped for the harsh climate and terrain of the Middle East, where the Anzac forces faced the armies of the Ottoman Empire(n)

programme.title: The Sinner
programme.desc: Long-held secrets are finally revealed as Cora delves into her past to recover her lost memories(n)

programme.title: Pappano's Classical Voices
programme.desc: Royal Opera House music director Antonio Pappano's comprehensive exploration of opera voice types continues as he focuses on the mezzo-soprano. As one of the lowest female voice types, and the one considered closest in tone to a woman's natural speaking voice, the mezzo-soprano only rarely plays the heroine part in operas. Typically, mezzo singers adopt the role of love rival, villainess, seductress, nurse, earth goddess or siren, but are given a chance to step into the spotlight in productions of Bizet's Carmen or Saint-Saen's Samson et Dalila. Antonio sets out to discover how the mezzo is able to convey a sense of evil in their singing style, while also unearthing redeeming qualities. Includes recordings of performances by Giuletta Simionato, Kathleen Ferrier, Marian Anderson, Shrley Verrett and Cecilia Bartoli among others(n)

programme.title: Secret Knowledge
programme.desc: The presenter tells the story of the 17th-century residence in Derbyshire - the pleasure palace of playboy Cavalier and ambitious courtier William Cavendish. Through a collection of artworks and items held within the castle, Lucy uncovers a colourful tale, capturing the tensions of England in the 1600s that would eventually lead the nation to civil war(n)

programme.title: Dan Cruickshank's Monuments of Remembrance
programme.desc: The extraordinary stories behind First World War cemeteries and memorials, including those at the Somme and Gallipoli, as well as those responsible for creating them(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: World War One at Home
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on life away from the battlefields, being shown as part of the World War One at Home season. Richie Woodhall tells the story of brothers Laurence and Egbert Cadbury, sons of chocolate company boss George. They both saw action in the First World War - yet only one of them was a fighter(n)

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Monty Halls reveals how the Great Barrier Reef is vitally linked to the rest of the planet in many ways. Creatures travel thousands of miles to visit, from tiger sharks, great whales and seabirds to the largest green-turtle gathering on Earth and rarely seen alien-like creatures, such as the tentacled nautilus. The reef is also affected by weather systems travelling from across the Pacific, including cyclones that bring destruction and chaos to the coral and the creatures that live on it(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of how 24-year-old Tracy Edwards became the skipper of the first all-female crew to enter the Whitbread Round the World Race in 1989. The film reveals how her male competitors thought an all-women crew would never make it, while the chauvinistic yachting press took bets on her failing, and potential sponsors rejected her, fearing the crew would die at sea and generate bad publicity(n)

programme.title: PQ17: An Arctic Convoy Disaster
programme.desc: Between 1941 and 1945, merchant ships escorted by vessels from the US, Canadian and British navies delivered four million tons of essential goods to the Soviet Union. In this documentary, Jeremy Clarkson retraces the journey of the convoy of 35 ships codenamed PQ17 from Iceland to the Russian port of Arkhangelsk in June and July 1942, revealing how the Anglo-American mission became one of the biggest naval disasters of the 20th century. Featuring testimony from men who served on the vessels(n)

programme.title: Genius of the Modern World
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes travels to Vienna on the trail of Sigmund Freud, a pioneer in the study of the human mind, whose psychoanalytic methods addressed emotional issues seldom even discussed in the 19th century. Talking to his patients inspired his radical understanding of the unconscious mind as a repository of hidden repressed emotions - and irrational primal desires(n)

programme.title: Timewatch
programme.desc: The story of Edward Mannock and James McCudden, the British fighter pilots who rose from modest backgrounds to become two of the country's greatest First World War aces. But as their tally of victories grew, so did their chances of dying. Timewatch recounts Mannock and McCudden's battle to survive against the odds - and the 90-year mystery surrounding their deaths(n)

programme.title: Pappano's Classical Voices
programme.desc: Antonio Pappano's tour of opera voice types draws to a suitable close with the lowest registers, the baritone and the bass. Typically, bass singers in opera find themselves portraying gods, demons, drunks, lechers and villains, and Antonio meets some of the Russian 'oktavists' who sing a whole octave lower than the standard bass. He also explores the baritone, the most common male voice type, but one that offers the singer a wide variety of roles. Includes recorded performances by celebrated practitioners including Bryn Terfel, John Tomlinson, Ferruccio Furlanetto, Feodor Chaliapin, Tito Gobbi, Boris Christoff and Ezio Pinza(n)

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Monty Halls reveals how the Great Barrier Reef is vitally linked to the rest of the planet in many ways. Creatures travel thousands of miles to visit, from tiger sharks, great whales and seabirds to the largest green-turtle gathering on Earth and rarely seen alien-like creatures, such as the tentacled nautilus. The reef is also affected by weather systems travelling from across the Pacific, including cyclones that bring destruction and chaos to the coral and the creatures that live on it(n)

programme.title: Live International Women's Football
programme.desc: Czech Republic v England (Kick-off 7.15pm). All the action from the friendly at Stadion Strelecky ostrov in Ceske Budejovice. This match comes just three days after the Lionesses faced Germany at Wembley, and head coach Phil Neville may opt to give run-outs to a number of fringe players who will be eager to impress against a team ranked 23 places below the visitors in the FIFA rankings(n)

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Vanessa Collingridge examines the life of Elizabeth Tudor, with a focus on how documentary TV and the BBC have looked at her legacy and interrogated her reign. Using archive stretching back over 60 years, her upbringing, her conflicts with enemies including Mary Queen of Scots, and her greatest victory against the Spanish Armada are investigated. The programme seeks to understand how the monarch created a legacy that people still live with today, and how it has changed throughout the centuries(n)

programme.title: Scuffles, Swagger & Shakespeare: The Hidden Story of English
programme.desc: John Gallagher examines how English became the world's most widely spoken language, examining the impact of William Shakespeare. During the playwright's lifetime, English was rarely spoken outside of Britain - but the beginnings of the British Empire would see it become a global language. The programme also examines what life was like for immigrants to Elizabethan Britain(n)

programme.title: Sleuths, Spies & Sorcerers: Andrew Marr's Paperback Heroes
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the genres of fiction that are the most popular with the general public, unpicking their conventions to show why they are such page-turners. He begins by looking at detective novels, profiling pioneers Agatha Christie, Dashiell Hammett and Ruth Rendell, and decoding set-pieces such as Hercule Poirot's drawing room denouements and the locked room mysteries of John Dickson Carr. With contributions by Ian Rankin, Sophie Hannah, Anthony Horowitz and Val McDermid(n)

programme.title: Make! Craft Britain
programme.desc: A programme celebrating all things handmade, following ordinary people as they learn new skills taught and take part in craft workshops led by inspirational teachers. In the first episode, two groups of novice crafters master the art of hooky rugmaking and traditional letterpress. Elsewhere, origami artist Sam Tsang demonstrates how to make something beautiful using a single sheet of paper(n)

programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Leicester City v Arsenal at the King Power Stadium and Chelsea v Crystal Palace at Stamford Bridge. Plus, action from Burnley v West Ham United at Turf Moor, Newcastle United v Bournemouth at St James' Park, Southampton v Everton at St Mary's Stadium, Tottenham Hotspur v Sheffield United at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, and Friday night's fixture between Norwich City and Watford at Carrow Road(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Remembrance Sunday
programme.desc: David Dimbleby presents live coverage of the ceremony in Whitehall, with the Queen watching proceedings from the balcony of the Foreign and Commonwealth Office as the Prince of Wales lays a wreath on behalf of the nation. On Horse Guards Parade, Sophie Raworth hears stories of courage and sacrifice from those taking part(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Thomas Plant help two teams find items of value at the Three Counties Showground in the spa town of Malvern, Worcestershire. Presented by Eric Knowles(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins marks Remembrance Sunday by visiting the National Memorial Arboretum near Lichfield in Staffordshire to see how its tranquil surroundings bring comfort and hope to those who have lost loved ones in the armed forces. Sean Fletcher chats to Chelsea Pensioner and Britain's Got Talent Winner Colin Thackery, who performs a special arrangement of the inspirational song You Raise Me Up(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents more updates, scores and goals from today's first-round fixtures(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is in Pembrokeshire with a couple who want not only to buy their first house together, but also need accommodation for holidaymakers and a horse(n)

programme.title: Gareth Edwards' Great Welsh Adventure
programme.desc: Rugby legend Gareth and wife Maureen reach the final leg of their round-Wales adventure by canal, one which has a personal connection for them. The narrowboat novices grew up at opposite ends of the same street in the village of Gwaun-cae-Gurwen, near Neath. As children, they would play in the areas by the derelict canals, but knew very little of them and their history. The Swansea canal and the Neath and Tennant canals reveal a story of industry that once dominated the area, getting coal and iron to the docks. Now virtually impassable, there are plans afoot to give them a new lease of life(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of Sainsbury's. After a challenging Christmas, the staff go all out to maximise profits with their flower sales over the Mother's Day weekend. Plans are also made to sell a birthday cake to celebrate the company's 150th year of trading, bringing in Claire Ptak, the creator of Harry and Meghan Markle's wedding cake, for ideas. Plus, last year CEO Mike Coupe announced a possible merger with Asda. Will industry regulators give the company the go-ahead to become the biggest supermarket in the country?(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker is with the Essex Wildlife Trust on Fingringhoe Wick - a newly created stretch of saltmarsh - as he helps the team do its first-ever winter fish survey, Margherita Taylor hears about a project to preserve the spirit of the Dig for Victory campaign, before joining Adrian Thomas from the RSPB to learn about the organisation's new sound archive. Adam Henson meets a farmer feeding his cows charcoal, and Tom Heap asks if enough is being done in rural areas to support veterans(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in South America, where animals must specialise if they are to carve out a niche. In Patagonia, a puma mother draws on a lifetime's experience to catch prey three times her weight, while rarely seen Andean bears clamber 30 metres into the cloud forest canopy to find elusive fruit. Poison dart frogs use ingenious methods to keep their tadpoles safe, anacondas stalk capuchin monkeys, and swifts make death-defying flights through Igauzu Falls on the border between Argentina and Brazil(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores taking part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. With music by West End star Luke Evans and a group dance marking Remembrance Sunday(n)

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra leaves the familiar surroundings of Jordan College and Oxford behind as she and Pantalaimon settle into their new life in London, but she is determined to try to find Roger and hopes that Mrs Coulter will be able to help in her mission. Meanwhile, Farder Coram and the Gyptians continue their search for the missing children and the elusive and mysterious Gobblers. Jack Thorne's adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen, Ruth Wilson and James Cosmo(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: On a Polish mission as part of the SOE, Harry grasps a second chance at saving Kasia from the horrors of Warsaw, while new mother Lois is surprised to be visited again by Vernon. Robina continues to appreciate her unlikely and surprising bond with Douglas and Jan, while Nancy promises Webster she will do all she can to help Albert(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including the top-of-the-table clash between Liverpool and Manchester City at Anfield. The Citizens pipped Liverpool to the title by one point last season, but it was the Reds who had set the pace so far this term. Plus, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Aston Villa, Manchester United v Brighton & Hove Albion and a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.desc: Lucy Owen finds out what is putting people off buying electric cars, while Omar Hamdi asks why customers who sign up for a Tastecard find it so difficult to cancel. Rachel Treadaway-Williams meets a couple who thought they were paying to reserve a property - only to discover they were victims of a scam(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The day of Louise and Sharon's party is here and Mel is determined that everything goes to plan. Sharon panics when Phil calls to say he is on his way back to Walford having heard about his wife's plan to sell the club. Mick and Linda worry as they take Ollie to hospital for his assessment, and Karen gets her hopes up when Keegan reveals he and Tiffany are moving out of Jack's. Jean struggles to contain her emotions as she opens up to Shirley about losing Daniel(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Justin Rowlatt investigates the aviation industry's plans to reduce carbon emissions and help save the planet, asking whether it is promising more than it is delivering. With cheap flights leading to a boom in passenger numbers, he hears claims that the industry is putting growth and profits before the environment(n)

programme.title: Gary Lineker: My Grandad's War
programme.desc: The Match of the Day presenter retraces the footsteps of his grandfather Stanley Abbs to explore a brutal but often overlooked chapter of the Second World War. Using the official war diary kept by Stanley's unit as a guide, Gary revisits the places where he was stationed and meets experts who help him piece together what happened to his grandfather during the Italian campaign. Along the way, Gary meets veterans who share first-hand testimonies to and help him understand the reality of combat in Italy(n)

programme.title: Tourist Trap
programme.desc: Wyn and Charlotte are proud to support a new feature film shooting in Wales, but discover it's not quite the sort of movie they thought it was. Nez calls in outside help to check the security of the agency's website and Elaine samples the very latest Welsh fusion cuisine at a pop-up dining experience. Guest starring Nick Helm and Cariad Lloyd(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Jock misses the first scan of his and Siobhan's baby, so her parents come up with a plan to adopt the child. The suggestion doesn't go down well with anyone, so to test Jock's ability to look after a child, Principal Walsh challenges him to take care of an egg for a full month - without losing or breaking it. Return of the comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley(n)

programme.desc: As the party continues, Sharon and Mel threaten to reveal each other's big secrets. While Lisa tries to make Mel see sense, Sharon sneaks out to meet Phil at the airport. Across the square, Chantelle bumps into Denise but is left confused when she mentions a fight Gray was involved in, and Linda and Mick take a trip down memory lane(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: A paranoid Jac feels she is the centre of hospital gossip when word spreads Emma is now living with Jonny. Elliot's concern for her grows, but feels unable to tell her his secret. Lofty resolves to put his new family first, but even the basics of parenting stir up trouble between him, Helen and Dom. Sacha struggles with his feelings for Essie, which seem doomed to go unrequited. Guest starring Vicky Entwistle (Coronation Street)(n)

programme.title: Gold Digger
programme.desc: Drama, starring Julia Ormond as a wealthy middle-aged woman who begins a relationship with a man nearly 20 years younger than she is just as her divorce is finalised. When he is introduced to her three adult children, suspicions are raised about his motivations. The action cuts between the early days of their relationship and the wedding day, which seems fated to end in disaster. With Ben Barnes and Jemima Rooper(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to life in Asia, the planet's most varied and extreme continent, which stretches from the Arctic Circle to the equator. Walruses gather in huge numbers in the frozen north and brown bears roam around remote Russian volcanoes, while the deep jungle provides sanctuary for the last few Sumatran rhino. programme.desc: Stevie is torn between his relationship with Poppy and his job with Lenny, Caitlin has a to-do list to fulfil on a very special birthday, and Bob just wants a day of peace, but family duties get in the way(n)

programme.title: Gold Digger
programme.desc: Drama, starring Julia Ormond as a wealthy middle-aged woman who begins a relationship with a man nearly 20 years younger than she is just as her divorce is finalised. When he is introduced to her three adult children, suspicions are raised about his motivations. The action cuts between the early days of their relationship and the wedding day, which seems fated to end in disaster. With Ben Barnes and Jemima Rooper(n)

programme.desc: A paranoid Jac feels she is the centre of hospital gossip when word spreads Emma is now living with Jonny. Elliot's concern for her grows, but feels unable to tell her his secret. Sacha struggles with his feelings for Essie, which seem doomed to go unrequited. Guest starring Vicky Entwistle (Coronation Street)(n)

programme.title: Love and Hate Crime
programme.desc: Two murders in Houston, Texas, that left baffled police thinking there was an Iranian hit squad at work. Ali Isran, who was convicted of the crimes, tells his story from death row, and the film follows the extraordinary twists and turns of the case(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Leicester City v Arsenal at the King Power Stadium and Chelsea v Crystal Palace at Stamford Bridge. The Foxes won last season's corresponding fixture 3-0, with second-half goals from Youri Tielemans and a Jamie Vardy brace securing all three points for Brendan Rodgers' side. Plus, action from Burnley v West Ham United at Turf Moor, Newcastle United v Bournemouth at St James' Park, Southampton v Everton at St Mary's Stadium, Tottenham Hotspur v Sheffield United at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, and Friday night's fixture between Norwich City and Watford at Carrow Road(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Remembrance Sunday
programme.desc: David Dimbleby presents live coverage of the ceremony in Whitehall, with the Queen watching proceedings from the balcony of the Foreign and Commonwealth Office as the Prince of Wales lays a wreath on behalf of the nation. On Horse Guards Parade, Sophie Raworth hears stories of courage and sacrifice from those taking part(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Thomas Plant help two teams find items of value at the Three Counties Showground in the spa town of Malvern, Worcestershire. Presented by Eric Knowles(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins marks Remembrance Sunday by visiting the National Memorial Arboretum near Lichfield in Staffordshire to see how its tranquil surroundings bring comfort and hope to those who have lost loved ones in the armed forces. Sean Fletcher chats to Chelsea Pensioner and Britain's Got Talent Winner Colin Thackery, who performs a special arrangement of the inspirational song You Raise Me Up(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents more updates, scores and goals from today's first-round fixtures(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Highlights of the programme in which Kate Humble and Ben Fogle report from behind the scenes at Longleat Safari Park in Wiltshire, featuring the vets trying to save a binturong with a mystery illness, and Jean Johansson meets the baby meerkats(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to life in Asia, the planet's most varied and extreme continent, which stretches from the Arctic Circle to the equator. Walruses gather in huge numbers in the frozen north and brown bears roam around remote Russian volcanoes, while the deep jungle provides sanctuary for the last few Sumatran rhino. There is also a glimpse of rarely seen creatures, from yeti-like golden snub-nosed monkeys in the mountain forests of China to a bizarre predator in the baking deserts of Iran(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker is with the Essex Wildlife Trust on Fingringhoe Wick - a newly created stretch of saltmarsh - as he helps the team do its first-ever winter fish survey, Margherita Taylor hears about a project to preserve the spirit of the Dig for Victory campaign, before joining Adrian Thomas from the RSPB to learn about the organisation's new sound archive. Adam Henson meets a farmer feeding his cows charcoal, and Tom Heap asks if enough is being done in rural areas to support veterans(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in South America, where animals must specialise if they are to carve out a niche. In Patagonia, a puma mother draws on a lifetime's experience to catch prey three times her weight, while rarely seen Andean bears clamber 30 metres into the cloud forest canopy to find elusive fruit. Poison dart frogs use ingenious methods to keep their tadpoles safe, anacondas stalk capuchin monkeys, and swifts make death-defying flights through Igauzu Falls on the border between Argentina and Brazil(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores taking part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. With music by West End star Luke Evans and a group dance marking Remembrance Sunday(n)

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra leaves the familiar surroundings of Jordan College and Oxford behind as she and Pantalaimon settle into their new life in London, but she is determined to try to find Roger and hopes that Mrs Coulter will be able to help in her mission. Meanwhile, Farder Coram and the Gyptians continue their search for the missing children and the elusive and mysterious Gobblers. Jack Thorne's adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen, Ruth Wilson and James Cosmo(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: On a Polish mission as part of the SOE, Harry grasps a second chance at saving Kasia from the horrors of Warsaw, while new mother Lois is surprised to be visited again by Vernon. Robina continues to appreciate her unlikely and surprising bond with Douglas and Jan, while Nancy promises Webster she will do all she can to help Albert(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including the top-of-the-table clash between Liverpool and Manchester City at Anfield. The Citizens pipped Liverpool to the title by one point last season, but it was the Reds who had set the pace so far this term. Plus, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Aston Villa, Manchester United v Brighton & Hove Albion and a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Motorcycling great Carl Fogarty, England netball star Eboni Usoro-Brown, Olympic hockey gold medallist Helen Richardson-Walsh and world champion swimmer Mark Foster join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood spends 24 hours sampling some of the US's most dangerous and demanding jobs, beginning by working as a correctional officer in a Texas jail(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell looks round a terraced house in Horden, Co Durham and a grade II listed building in Whitehaven, Cumbria. Dion Dublin is in Evesham, Worcestershire(n)

programme.title: Two Minutes Silence
programme.desc: Pause to remember those who have given their lives in war(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A bogus injury scam is foiled as football supporters fail to see the CCTV cameras on board a bus, and a bride-to-be comes unstuck over a claim for a lost engagement ring(n)

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: The programme follows police as they seize a Mercedes from a drug dealer who had not paid back the money he made from crime. Angellica Bell and Rav Wilding present(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: An ex-soldier struggling with motherhood puts Emma in a difficult position rather than tell her partner how she really feels, while Jimmi has some advice for a troubled Daniel(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which contestants must choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Leicestershire to help a couple look for a property on a £750,000 budget, and pays a visit to an arts and crafts cottage that is a time capsule from a bygone era(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and the team transforms items reclaimed from a tip in Witley, Surrey, including a copper hopper funnel, a bedside table and an old mirror and washstand(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and James Braxton battle for profits among the orchards of Kent, before heading to an auction in Washington, West Sussex(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, with former US First Lady and Secretary of State Hillary Clinton(n)

programme.title: Barra on the Bann
programme.desc: Weather presenter Barra Best travels along the Bann, meeting people who live, work and relax on Northern Ireland's longest river. He begins his 100-mile journey at the source of the Bann on Slieve Muck in the Mourne Mountains, before staying overnight at an eco farm, and learns about a unique project to reintroduce red kites to the region(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The day of Louise and Sharon's party is here and Mel is determined that everything goes to plan. Sharon panics when Phil calls to say he is on his way back to Walford having heard about his wife's plan to sell the club. Mick and Linda worry as they take Ollie to hospital for his assessment, and Karen gets her hopes up when Keegan reveals he and Tiffany are moving out of Jack's. Jean struggles to contain her emotions as she opens up to Shirley about losing Daniel(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Justin Rowlatt investigates the aviation industry's plans to reduce carbon emissions and help save the planet, asking whether it is promising more than it is delivering. With cheap flights leading to a boom in passenger numbers, he hears claims that the industry is putting growth and profits before the environment(n)

programme.title: On The Frontline
programme.desc: Using first-hand testimonies and extensive archive footage, this film documents the experiences of police during the Troubles, when 319 RUC officers were killed and 9,000 injured. As well as dealing with everyday crime the RUC was at the forefront of the state's efforts to tackle terrorism, dealing with the aftermath of bombings and paramilitary shootings(n)

programme.title: Gary Lineker: My Grandad's War
programme.desc: The Match of the Day presenter retraces the footsteps of his grandfather Stanley Abbs to explore a brutal but often overlooked chapter of the Second World War. Using the official war diary kept by Stanley's unit as a guide, Gary revisits the places where he was stationed and meets experts who help him piece together what happened to his grandfather during the Italian campaign. Along the way, Gary meets veterans who share first-hand testimonies to and help him understand the reality of combat in Italy(n)

programme.title: Pop Goes Northern Ireland
programme.desc: A look back at the events of 1991, soundtracked by pop hits from the time. In February, the IRA launched an attack when a mortar bomb exploded in the garden of 10 Downing Street(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Jock misses the first scan of his and Siobhan's baby, so her parents come up with a plan to adopt the child. The suggestion doesn't go down well with anyone, so to test Jock's ability to look after a child, Principal Walsh challenges him to take care of an egg for a full month - without losing or breaking it. Return of the comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Citizen Khan star Adil Ray hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Ivo Graham and journalist Helen Lewis joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host chats to Oscar-winner Olivia Colman and Helena Bonham Carter, who are joining Netflix drama The Crown as the Queen and Princess Margaret respectively. Black Panther star Chadwick Boseman discusses his role in new action thriller 21 Bridges, while comedian and actor Richard Ayoade talks about his book Ayoade on Top. Plus, Niall Horan performs Nice to Meet Ya(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood takes on his toughest challenge yet, working as a paramedic in Austin, Texas. His mentor is Cassi Lydon, a veteran paramedic with 14 years' service(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a two-bedroom flat with rental potential in Catford, a terraced house in Dover, and a quirky terraced house in Accrington(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A driver's personal injury claim is rumbled when CCTV footage reveals that he was never even in the car, and an opportunistic bus passenger comes unstuck(n)

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: A massive police operation in Liverpool leads to a series of arrests of criminals, all of whom have made money illegally, but now the police want it back. We also see how Asset Recovery teams in the South West arrest a drugs gang and then seize the designer contents of their wardrobe - literally taking the shirts off their backs. Plus we meet the boxing club in Tuebrook who have had funding from money local police took off criminals(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Kate Bliss help two teams look for items in Stamford Meadows, Lincolnshire, while Natasha Raskin Sharp has advice on how to identify different kinds of wood(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate when ladies' man and rowing club member Guy Sweetman is found dead in the river during the annual Midsomer Regatta. They soon discover the elite exterior of the club hides a world of money problems and sexual jealousy and the victim had a number of enemies who might have wanted him dead. Guest starring Olympic rower Steve Redgrave and Diana Quick(n)

programme.title: The X Factor: Celebrity
programme.desc: Dermot O'Leary hosts the next stage in the star-studded version of the singing contest, which features further live performances in London. The remaining acts have got through the initial rounds in Los Angeles and the judges' deliberation, and now must battle it out for the chance to be crowned champion and win a record deal. They will have the support and coaching of the judges, with Simon Cowell taking charge of the Groups, Nicole Scherzinger mentoring the Overs, and Louis Walsh looking after the Unders(n)

programme.title: Back to Mine
programme.desc: Bachelor Cal goes looking for love by checking out the houses of three potential dates in the North West - and this former professional footballer appears to have a huge choice when it comes to picking one to take out on a date. There is a pop music lover, a fitness fanatic and the 'Beyonce of the caravan park'. But which one will he want to score a date with?(n)

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince (2009)
programme.desc: The young wizard is given the task of uncovering the secrets of Lord Voldemort's past as the evil sorcerer's henchmen cause havoc in both the magical and Muggle worlds. With the help of headmaster Dumbledore, Harry ventures into the memories of those who knew the Dark Lord during his early years, hoping to gain an insight into the secret behind his apparent immortality. However, Voldemort's influence extends much closer to home than expected, as Harry's rival Malfoy is chosen to carry out a sinister mission. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Daniel Radcliffe, Michael Gambon, Emma Watson, Rupert Grint, Tom Felton and Alan Rickman(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking Strictly Come Dancing professional Oti Mabuse, tennis coach Judy Murray and actor-comedian John Thomson to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. One of them will make it through to the Super Catchphrase round, where they could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice(n)

programme.title: Ant & Dec's DNA Journey
programme.desc: Anthony McPartlin and Declan Donnelly may think they know who they are, but in this two-part documentary, the popular presenters uncover their surprising and emotional family histories, using a combination of DNA and genealogy. The shock revelations include military men in both of their families - one a medal-winning war hero, the other a deserter. And as well as delving into the past, Ant and Dec also get to meet DNA relatives they never knew existed(n)

programme.title: Charles: 50 Years a Prince
programme.desc: Fifty years ago in Caernarfon at his investiture, Prince Charles embarked on a unique relationship with a country that has shaped his life and work. While acknowledging a sometimes troubled and turbulent history, this documentary celebrates Wales through the eyes of a prince who has made the role his own. Narrated by Luke Evans(n)

programme.title: Fifty Shades of Green with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: To celebrate half a century spent in gardens around the world, the broadcaster visits his favourite horticultural locations around the UK with friends and experts who share his passions. He meets food writer Mary Berry at his favourite vegetable plot, goes inside Prince Charles' garden at Highgrove, takes a tour of Griff Rhys Jones' epic space and and strokes the hallowed turf on Wimbledon's Centre Court. Alan also returns to the allotment where, as a toddler, he was first introduced to gardening(n)

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince (2009)
programme.desc: The young wizard is given the task of uncovering the secrets of Lord Voldemort's past as the evil sorcerer's henchmen cause havoc in both the magical and Muggle worlds. With the help of headmaster Dumbledore, Harry ventures into the memories of those who knew the Dark Lord during his early years, hoping to gain an insight into the secret behind his apparent immortality. However, Voldemort's influence extends much closer to home than expected, as Harry's rival Malfoy is chosen to carry out a sinister mission. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Daniel Radcliffe, Michael Gambon, Emma Watson, Rupert Grint, Tom Felton and Alan Rickman(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase

programme.title: Ant & Dec's DNA Journey
programme.desc: Anthony McPartlin and Declan Donnelly may think they know who they are, but in this two-part documentary, the popular presenters uncover their surprising and emotional family histories, using a combination of DNA and genealogy. The shock revelations include military men in both of their families - one a medal-winning war hero, the other a deserter. And as well as delving into the past, Ant and Dec also get to meet DNA relatives they never knew existed(n)

programme.title: Charles: 50 Years a Prince
